body:
Hi Mom,

Quick update since we talked last week — I checked the schedule Dex posted at Alchemy and I'm off Thursday like I thought, so I'm blocking the morning for the zine essay before I lose my nerve on it. Figured I'd tell you so you don't call at 10am wondering why I'm not answering, ha.

Also wanted to ask — you mentioned maybe swinging by Providence sometime this month? If Thursday works I could actually do lunch after I write, since I won't have to run to a shift. No pressure, just thought I'd offer while I have an actual free day for once.

Love you,
Jenny

Hi Jenny,

Thursday lunch sounds lovely — let's say noon, that little place near the bookshop you and Joon like? I can drive up, your father's got a work thing anyway so he won't miss me.

Glad you're actually protecting that writing time instead of letting it get swallowed by shifts and everyone else's errands. You've been saying you'd do that for months, so good for you for following through. Tell Dex I said hi, and give Reese a nudge to call me back, she's been dodging me all week.

See you Thursday,
Mom

Hi Mom,

Open mic went well last night — packed house, Dex ran the sign-up sheet like a bouncer, and somebody's cousin did twenty minutes of stand-up about her cat's oral surgery. Wanted to say thanks for coming out, I know Tuesdays are rough for you.

Also, if you still have that folding table from the garage, I could use it for the zine table next month. No rush.

Love,
Jen

Jen,

So glad I went — that cat bit was somehow both unhinged and genuinely funny, tell Dex he's a natural at crowd control. The table's still in the garage, your dad can drop it off whenever, just say the word.

How'd the sound levels work out this time? Last time you mentioned the mic kept cutting during the poetry reader.

Love you,
Mom

Mom,

Sound's fixed — Joon rewired the DI box between shifts so no more cutting out. Table would be great this weekend if Dad's headed this way anyway, no special trip needed.

Also can't do our usual Sunday call this week, doing the lentil soup batch cook late and then Reese wants to video chat about something. Thursday instead?

Love,
Jen

Hey Mom,

Quick one — you asked how the zine run with Joon went. We got About 40 copies of the new issue into Riffraff and two spots on the East Side, plus a stack left at Alchemy by the register. Joon's van made the whole thing way less of a slog than hauling boxes on the bus like last time.

We're doing another batch drop in a couple weeks if Riffraff sells through. I'll keep you posted.

Love,
Jen

Hi honey,

That's wonderful, I'm glad the van made it easier on you two. Did the bookshop folks say anything about the writing itself, or just logistics? I'd love to actually read this issue if you're willing to send a copy — your father keeps asking too, though I think he mostly wants to brag about it to the guys at his shop.

Also, are you and Joon splitting gas money evenly, or is that something you two need to sort out better? Just don't want you carrying more than your share on that.

Love,
Mom

Hey Mom,

Gas is fine, we just alternate who fills up — not worth overthinking. As for feedback, one of the Riffraff folks flagged my essay on the coffee shop union drive as the standout, which felt good to hear out loud in a bookshop instead of just from Dex over the espresso machine.

I'll mail you and Dad a copy this week, I have extras from the batch that didn't get placed. Might staple a little note in it too.

Talk soon,
Jen

Hi Jen,

That makes me so happy to hear, and please do send the note along — your father will probably read it twice. I'm proud of you for keeping this going alongside everything else, the shop shifts and the writing time you carve out. It's not nothing to sustain a project like this.

Let me know when the next distribution run is, I like picturing you and Joon out doing it.

Love always,
Mom

Hi Mom,

Quick follow-up from Sunday's lentil situation — turned out great, if a little biblical in quantity. Joon and I portioned it into what I'm pretty sure is every mason jar in the house, so if you want the recipe (the one with the charred lemon, not the one that made Nia cough), I can send it over before next batch day.

Also wanted to ask — did you want me to bring some over when I see you, or are you and Dad still doing the low-sodium thing? Don't want to hand Dad a jar of sodium-bomb soup and get blamed for his blood pressure.

Love,
Jen

Hi Jenny,

Glad it turned out well — your father actually asked about it, which for him counts as enthusiasm. Yes please on the recipe, and yes, still doing low-sodium, so maybe just go easy on the salt in a separate small batch for us if it's not too much trouble.

Also, if you're coming by, bring a jar for Reese too — she mentioned she hasn't eaten a vegetable since Tuesday, which worries me more than she'd like.

Love,
Mom

Hi Michelle,

Following up on the ticket from last week — I'm still getting that invalid_grant error when the script tries to refresh the OAuth token for the Gmail API. It's the thing Joon and I use to blast out the zine mailing list before distribution runs, so it's a little urgent since we've got a bookshop drop this weekend and I'd rather not hand-type forty emails on my day off.

I double-checked the credentials.json against what's in the Cloud Console like you suggested, and the client ID matches. Is this a scope issue? Or did a token just expire because we haven't run it since the last batch?

Thanks for staying on this with me.

Jen

Hi Jen,

Good news, I think I found it — the refresh token gets invalidated if the app's been sitting unused past the testing-mode expiry window, which sounds like exactly your situation. Since your OAuth consent screen is still in "Testing" status, tokens expire after 7 days of inactivity. Two options: publish the app (even to just yourself as a test account won't fix it, it needs to move out of testing), or re-run the auth flow to mint a fresh token before every batch.

For a script you're only running occasionally around distribution runs, I'd honestly just re-auth each time — takes thirty seconds. Delete the old token.json, run the script, click through the browser prompt once, and you're set.

Let me know if it throws anything else. And good luck with the bookshop run, tell Joon I said hi.

Mom

Hey,

That fixed it — deleted the token file, re-authed, and the mailer went out clean this morning before my shift at Alchemy. Joon's already loading the car for the bookshop run so timing worked out perfectly.

Going to add a calendar reminder so we re-auth before every batch instead of finding out the hard way at 6am. Thanks for walking me through it, appreciate you.

Love you, talk Sunday,
Jen

Hi Michelle,

Following up on ticket #4471 about the Google Calendar API OAuth token issue. I patched in the refresh token like your last email suggested, and the little script that pulls my shift schedule from Alchemy's scheduling doc into my personal calendar worked for about four days before throwing invalid_grant again. This is the thing I built so I stop double-booking zine distribution runs with Joon against clopening shifts, so it's a bit more than a hobby project at this point.

Could you check whether the app I registered is still stuck in "Testing" publishing status? I recall that caps the refresh token life at seven days, which lines up suspiciously well with the timing.

Thanks,
Jennifer

Hello Jennifer,

Thanks for the detail — that's exactly the right instinct. I checked and yes, your OAuth consent screen is still in Testing mode, which does cap refresh tokens at 7 days for any test account, yourself included. You have two options: move the app to "In production" (no verification needed if you're only requesting the calendar.events scope), or just add your own account explicitly under Test Accounts so it doesn't expire silently.

Given it's a single-user personal tool, I'd recommend the production toggle since it's the simpler fix and avoids you having to re-auth weekly.

Let me know if you'd like a walkthrough of the Cloud Console screen, happy to send screenshots.

Best,
Michelle

Hi Michelle,

That worked, thank you. Flipped it to production status this morning before my shift and the token's held so far. Small victory, but I'll take it — this is the same week I'm trying to get a batch of lentil soup portioned out on Sunday and keep the open mic night lineup straight, so one less thing silently breaking is genuinely nice.

One follow-up: is there a way to widen the scope later if I want the script to also create events (not just read), or would that require going through Google's verification review since it'd touch more of the calendar?

Thanks again,
Jennifer

Hi Jennifer,

Glad the token issue is resolved. To answer your question: calendar.events is actually a "sensitive" scope rather than "restricted," so as a single-user internal tool you can add write access without going through the full verification review — that's reserved for apps requesting broader access or serving many external users. You'll just see an "unverified app" warning screen when you re-authorize, which you can click through since it's your own project.

If you do add the write scope, you'll need to re-consent once, so expect one more OAuth prompt the next time you run the script.

Feel free to reopen this thread if anything else comes up — good luck with the soup batch and the open mic lineup.

Best,
Michelle

Hi Michelle,

Following up on ticket #4471 about the Drive API quota errors — the 403 'User Rate Limit Exceeded' is still popping up when the sync script tries to push more than ten files at once. This is the little tool Joon and I use to mirror the zine's print-ready PDFs into the shared folder before our bookshop distribution runs, so when it chokes I end up uploading everything by hand between opening shifts at Alchemy, which is its own kind of tragicomedy.

I added the exponential backoff snippet from your last email, but it's still failing on the same batch size, not smoothing out over time like I'd expect if it were just throttling. Is there a per-project quota I need to bump in the console, or is this a scopes issue with the service account? Happy to send the error log if that helps.

Thanks for sticking with me on this one.

Jennifer

Hi Jennifer,

Thanks for the details — that's genuinely useful. I pulled up #4471, and this does look like a quota configuration issue rather than the backoff logic. Service accounts default to a fairly low per-minute write quota unless the project has requested an increase, and ten simultaneous file pushes plus metadata calls will burst right past it. Backoff won't smooth that out because you're hitting a hard ceiling, not just contention.

Two things to try: first, in the Cloud Console under APIs & Services > Quotas, filter for Drive API and check the 'Queries per minute per user' value — if it's still at default, request an increase and it usually clears in a day or two. Second, in the meantime, batch your uploads in groups of three or four with a short pause between groups rather than firing all ten at once; that alone often resolves it without waiting on the quota bump.

Go ahead and send the error log anyway so I can confirm it's a 403 quota code and not something scope-related hiding underneath. I'd rather rule it out than have you hand-uploading zine PDFs on your break.

Best,
Michelle

Hi Michelle,

Following up on the ticket from last week — the Etsy API key you helped me regenerate is still throwing that 401 error whenever the shop tries to sync new zine listings. I tried re-authenticating twice, cleared the app permissions, even had Joon look at it since he's less allergic to reading docs than I am. Same result.

Is there a rate-limit thing happening, or did something break on the connected-apps side after that last Etsy update? I've got a distribution run with Joon this weekend and would love to have new listings live before then.

Thanks for staying on this,
Jennifer

Hi Jennifer,

Thanks for the details — that helps narrow it down. Looking at the logs, the 401 is actually coming from a scope mismatch: the API key was reissued with read-only permissions instead of read-write, which would explain why authentication succeeds but the listing sync fails silently until it hits that call. I've corrected the scope on our end and pushed a refreshed key to your developer dashboard.

Could you try disconnecting and reconnecting the app once more? It should prompt you to accept the new permission set this time. If it still fails after that, send me the exact timestamp of a failed attempt and I'll pull the raw request log.

Hope the zine batch goes smoothly this weekend either way.

Best,
Michelle

Hi Michelle,

That did it — reconnected, accepted the new scope, and the listings synced right up. Joon and I actually got the new zine batch loaded before heading out on the bookshop run, so good timing all around.

Appreciate you digging into the scope issue instead of just telling me to clear my cache for the fifth time. Marking this one resolved on my end — will open a new ticket if anything else pops up.

Thanks again,
Jennifer

Hi Mom,

Follow-up on what we talked about Sunday — I actually stuck to the plan this week. Checked the shift schedule the second it went up, saw I've got Wednesday off, and blocked the morning for writing before I let the day get away from me. Feels like a small thing but it's the first time in weeks I've protected it on purpose instead of just hoping it happens.

Still working out whether mornings or afternoons are better for the zine stuff, but at least the block exists now. Talk soon.

Love,
Jennifer

Hi Jen,

Glad to hear it — you've mentioned wanting to do this for a while, so it's good you're actually testing it instead of just thinking about it. Mornings tend to be better for that kind of focus, in my experience, before the day starts making demands on you. Your father would say the same, though he'd say it while already halfway distracted by something else.

How's the writing itself going, content-wise? And are Dex and Joon still on board with the zine timeline, or has that shifted?

Love,
Mom

Hey Mom,

Writing's slow but steady — got a decent chunk done Wednesday before my brain wandered off. Joon and I are doing the bookshop distribution run again this weekend, so that'll eat into it a little, but I'd rather have the zine actually out in the world than perfectly polished and stuck on my laptop forever.

The open mic at Alchemy this week helped too, oddly — hearing other people read half-finished things made me less precious about my own.

Talk this weekend?

Love,
Jennifer

Hi Jen,

That sounds like real progress, and I like that you're letting the imperfect version count. Say hi to Joon for me, and tell Dex I said the open mic idea was a good one.

Call Sunday works — I'll be around after your soup-prep, so no rush. Proud of you for holding onto the writing time even with everything else pulling at it.

Love,
Mom

Hey Mom,

Just a follow-up on open mic — it went better than the sign-up sheet suggested it would (three separate guys named Chris, all doing spoken word about their exes, truly a genre unto itself). Dex closed with that Talking Heads cover on the house ukulele and got the loudest response of the night, which feels like a sentence I never expected to type with a straight face.

I read three poems off the new zine, the one Joon and I have been putting together — went fine, nobody heckled, low bar but I'll take it. Wish you could've been there. Might do it again next month if Alchemy keeps the slot open. How's Dad's knee doing?

Love,
Jenny

Jenny,

I'm so glad it went well — three Chrises doing exes-themed poetry sounds like something out of a sitcom, I laughed out loud reading that. Tell Dex I'm impressed, tell him it's about time he shared his ukulele talents with the world.

Your father's knee is better, he's back to puttering in the garden against doctor's orders, you know how he is. I'd love to come to the next one if the timing works — let me know the date as soon as you have it, I'll ask Reese if she wants to make a weekend of it.

Proud of you and Joon for getting that zine out into the world, by the way. Send me a copy when you can.

Love,
Mom

Hey Mom,

Quick one — is it cool if I swing by Sunday afternoon and grab a box or two of your old paperbacks for the bookshop distro run? Joon and I are doing the zine drop-off across a few Providence spots (Riffraff, that new place on Wickenden) and figured your shelf could use the thinning out you keep talking about.

Also lentil soup batch-cook is that morning so I'll probably smell like cumin when I show up, fair warning.

Love,
Jen

Hi honey,

Of course, take whatever you want — there's a stack by the reading chair I've been meaning to donate anyway, mostly Le Carré and some water-damaged poetry collections. Your father says hi and wants to know if the zine this issue has any of his pun suggestions in it, he's still bitter you cut the one about the barista and the bean counter.

Come by around 2, I'll have coffee on. Bring Joon in if he wants, I have extra of that soup you like too, the rosemary bread thing.

Love you,
Mom

Ha, tell Dad his pun got cut for pacing reasons, not quality — mostly. We'll be there around 2, Joon's driving since my car's still making that noise.

Thanks for the books, seriously, half our shelf space at the distro spots is paperback trades so this helps a lot. See you Sunday.

Love,
Jen

Hey Mom — quick one. Doing the lentil soup batch again this Sunday, same recipe as always, but I'm out of bay leaves and can't remember if you use the smoked paprika or the regular in yours. Also did you ever figure out that trick for keeping the lentils from going mushy when you reheat leftovers? Mine always turn to mush by day three.

Hi honey, use the smoked paprika, it's the whole reason your father actually eats his vegetables without complaining. For the mushy problem — cook the lentils about five minutes less than you think you need, they keep cooking in the soup as it cools. Also undersalt slightly if you're freezing portions, salt gets weird in the freezer. How's the zine going, did you and Joon get the new issue to the shop yet?

Bay leaves and smoked paprika, got it, thank you. Zine's almost done, Joon and I are doing the bookshop run Thursday if the printer cooperates. Soup update: undersalting worked, no mush, Dex declared it 'structurally sound' which from him is basically a Michelin star. One more thing — do you blanch the carrots separately or just throw them in raw with everything else?

Ha, tell Dex I'm flattered. Just throw the carrots in raw with everything else, they'll soften plenty in the simmer time and you keep more of the flavor that way — blanching first just washes it out into the water you're going to dump anyway. Glad the freezing trick worked. Let me know how the bookshop run goes, and give my love to Joon. Talk Sunday when you call Reese, maybe?
